headline: Tesla deliveries surge 25%, crushing estimates
detail: Tesla's blowout quarter signals consumer demand for high-ticket discretionary is healthier than feared. That's broadly bullish for growth and tech sentiment - the names you're concentrated in directly benefit from this risk-on read. It also lifts the EV/auto supply chain narrative which overlaps with chip demand.
rec: No direct holding to add here, but this supports holding QQQ and SMH through current levels - no action needed, just confirms the risk-on backdrop.

headline: Russia massive strike on Ukraine, Poland scrambles jets
detail: Serious escalation in Eastern Europe. Historically these events cause short-term risk-off spikes that hit growth/tech first and hardest. On margin, even a one-day gap down stings. This is the key tail risk sitting under today's bullish tape.
rec: No trade today, but this is exactly why you keep stops live on leveraged positions like SOXL and SPAL - geopolitical shocks hit fast and don't wait for you to log in.

headline: Brookfield building AI data centers in London financial district
detail: More institutional capital flowing into AI infrastructure globally. This is medium-term bullish for chip and semiconductor names - your SMH and SOXL holdings are the direct beneficiaries of sustained data center buildout demand.
rec: No action today - this confirms the secular thesis behind your chip exposure but doesn't give a specific entry trigger right now.

headline: Ford Q2 sales drop 10.3%, EV demand falling
detail: Ford's weakness is a contrast to Tesla's blowout - it suggests EV demand is brand-specific, not sector-wide. Falling EV demand at legacy makers could pressure auto-adjacent chip names slightly, but your book is more AI/cloud chip focused than auto chip focused.
rec: No action - this doesn't directly hit your holdings, but watch if it creates broader semiconductor sentiment drag on SMH intraday.
